metadata.description: Historical configuration disasters database for risk pattern recognition

incidents.name: CrowdStrike Falcon Kernel Driver Failure
incidents.trigger: Unsafe kernel driver configuration deployed globally
incidents.outcome: Millions of Windows machines failed to boot
lessons_learned: Kernel configs must use staged rollouts
Global blast radius must be assessed

incidents.name: AWS DynamoDB DNS TTL Misconfiguration Outage
incidents.trigger: DNS TTL and client-side timeout configuration reduced aggressively
incidents.outcome: Widespread DynamoDB request failures due to DNS resolution timeouts
incidents.root_cause: Overly aggressive DNS TTL and timeout configuration causing retry storms
lessons_learned: DNS TTL changes must be tested under load
Timeout reductions can amplify retry storms
Global services require conservative DNS settings

incidents.name: Intel Vmin Firmware Voltage Shift Instability
incidents.trigger: Firmware update modified minimum voltage thresholds (Vmin)
incidents.outcome: Intermittent system crashes and degraded performance across affected CPUs
incidents.root_cause: Unsafe voltage configuration introduced via firmware update
lessons_learned: Firmware config changes require long-term stability testing
Hardware-level changes can cause delayed failures
Voltage margins should be adjusted conservatively
